LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT LEE & GEORGIOU'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE THIS ACTION WITH LEE & GEORGIOU V. 365 LIBERTY LLC, CGC-19-579323 IS DENIED. WHILE THE TWO CASES INVOLVE SOME OVERLAPPING PARTIES AND ISSUES, THEY ARE AT DIFFERENT STAGES AND PRESENT DIFFERING PROCEDURAL POSTURES. THE OTHER CASE WAS FILED IN SEPTEMBER 2019, ARBITRATION AND DISCOVERY IS ONGOING, AND THERE IS A JAMS ARBITRATION SET FOR JANUARY 2021. THE PLAINTIFF IN THIS ACTION (WHO PURCHASED ONE OF THE TWO ADJOINING PROPERTIES) IS NOT A PARTY TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT, AND IS NOT CONTRACTUALLY OBLIGATED TO ARBITRATE WITH THE PARTIES THAT ACTION. IN THIS ACTION, IN CONTRAST, THE PARTIES HAVE RECENTLY STIPULATED TO THE APPOINTMENT OF A SPECIAL MASTER. UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES, THE COURT FINDS THAT CONSOLIDATION WOULD NOT BE PRACTICABLE AND WOULD NOT SERVE THE INTERESTS OF JUDICIAL ECONOMY. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.

Hearing On Motion To Consolidate Actions
Off Calendar Oct-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael Charles Miller of the law offices of Michael C. Miller (lawofficemichaelcmiller@gmail.com; collect1@earthlink.net) appears as counsel for moving/opposing party, plaintiff, LUMINOUS ION LLC

Attorney, Anela Lee of the law offices of [ ] () appears as counsel for moving/opposing party

Attorney, Richard Elder of the law offices of [ ] (relder@lubinolson.com) appears as counsel for moving/opposing party

Attorney, Robert P. Hamilton of the law offices of GOODMAN NEUMAN HAMILTON LLP (RHamilton@gnhllp.com) appears as counsel for moving/opposing party

Attorney Douglas Akay of the law offices of Douglas Akau (dnakay@akaylaw.com) for moving/part, LEE & GEORGIOU INC. DBA GEORGIOU CONST

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant and Cross-Complainant Lee & Georgiou's motion to consolidate this action with Lee & Georgiou v. 365 Liberty LLC, CGC-19-579323 is denied. While the two cases involve some overlapping parties and issues, they are at different stages and present differing procedural postures. The other case was filed in September 2019, arbitration and discovery is ongoing, and there is a JAMS arbitration set for January 2021. The plaintiff in this action (who purchased one of the two adjoining properties) is not a party to the arbitration agreement, and is not contractually obligated to arbitrate with the parties that action. In this action, in contrast, the parties have recently stipulated to the appointment of a special master. Under the circumstances, the court finds that consolidation would not be practicable and would not serve the interests of judicial economy. Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com.
